meta.title: BMW B38 / B48 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost

hero.h1: BMW B38 / B48 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The B38 and B48 are BMW's modern modular turbo petrol engines — generally reliable, but coolant system issues are well known. Backed by an estimated 350+ real UK enquiries in 2025 [ESTIMATED].

rows.value: Exclusive to BMW (also Mini, not covered here)

rows.value: One-line verdict: "The B38/B48 are BMW's modern turbo petrols — coolant system issues are the main concern — buy with full service history and budget for water pump replacement." (Clause 1: 8 words. Clause 2: 7 words. Clause 3: 9 words. Total: 24 words.)

rows.metric: Best For: Daily drivers who want a responsive, economical petrol engine with good reliability.
rows.value: Avoid If: You're looking for the ultimate performance – the B58 six‑cylinder is the better choice for enthusiasts.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting a B38/B48 typically takes 8–12 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [ESTIMATED].

cta.label: → Compare BMW B48 engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: Cost — "How much does a BMW B48 engine replacement cost?"
items.answer: A reconditioned B48 typically costs £2,500–£4,000 supply only [ESTIMATED], varying by power output and specification. Fitting adds £600–£1,100 depending on labour rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

items.question: Reliability — "Is the BMW B48 a reliable engine?"
items.answer: The B48 scores 4★ out of 5 with 88% confidence [ESTIMATED]. It's generally considered reliable, with coolant leaks (water pump, expansion tank) being the most common issue. Proper servicing is key to longevity.

items.question: Common failure — "What is the most common BMW B48 failure?"
items.answer: Water pump and thermostat housing leaks are common, often requiring replacement around 60,000–100,000 miles. PCV valve failure can also cause oil consumption. Early B48s had some crankcase ventilation issues [PATTERN DATA].

items.question: Fitment — "Which BMW models use the B48?"
items.answer: The B48 powers the 1 Series (F20/F40 120i, M135i), 2 Series (F22 M235i), 3 Series (F30/G20 320i/330i), 4 Series (420i/430i), 5 Series (520i/530i), X1, X2, X3, and X4, across 2014–present.

items.question: Longevity — "How long does the BMW B48 last?"
items.answer: With regular oil and coolant changes, and proactive water pump replacement, the B48 can exceed 150,000–200,000 miles. Many examples are still on original engines well past 100,000 miles with no major issues.
